proved: The public providerGenerate wrapper accepts ProviderResearchTaskRequest rather than a raw provider generation body.
The public request contains question, optional task instructions, and labeled source excerpts; it contains no approval, disclosure-category, or detached source-ID fields.
Rust serializes the actual research payload, derives content categories only from present fields, and derives run-record source IDs only from the source objects.
Duplicate snapshot IDs, control characters in source labels, empty content, excessive field sizes, and invalid identifiers fail before the native dialog, vault, or network.
The derived provider input contains the same question, snapshot identity, label, and excerpt used to author disclosure/provenance metadata.
All 65 Rust tests and the Rust-to-TypeScript provider-run record interoperability check pass without external network or real credentials.
The structural audit rejects reintroduction of caller-authored approval, categories, or detached provenance.
The compiled MCP publishes native-disclosure-research-envelope status while retaining twelve tools and no provider-generation authority.
notProved: A product domain workflow selects the correct frozen editor/Drive snapshots for the envelope.
A live provider receives and interprets the JSON research envelope as intended.
The native disclosure dialog has been clicked in a packaged app with a real research task.
Streaming, tools, retries, model discovery, billing, or adversarial multi-provider orchestration.
Source-label/excerpt privacy is appropriate for a particular research protocol or legal regime.
